findings.description: Reference to non-existent Section 6 for security considerations
findings.evidence: Line 37: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' — Document only has 5 sections.
findings.suggestion: Remove reference to missing section and either add security section or remove the reference entirely

findings.description: Internal contradiction: document claims 'all pipeline stages run in parallel' but then states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins'
findings.evidence: Line 5: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Line 21: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Clarify the actual execution model — either all truly parallel (with eventual consistency) or sequential dependencies where they exist

findings.description: Stage 4 (Monitoring) is sparsely documented compared to Stages 1-3
findings.evidence: Stage 1: ~6 lines, Stage 2: ~7 lines, Stage 3: ~6 lines, Stage 4: ~4 lines. Stage 4 only mentions what's exposed, not what it does.
findings.suggestion: Expand Stage 4 to match detail level of other stages; explain monitoring responsibilities, alert mechanisms, and operational workflows

findings.description: Unsupported performance claim: '99.9% latency reduction' lacks any supporting calculation or source material
findings.evidence: Line 29: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.' — No explanation of how this figure was derived.
findings.suggestion: Either provide calculation/benchmark methodology or soften claim to qualitative statement

findings.description: Performance benchmark lacks methodology and source material
findings.evidence: Lines 40-41: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' — No reference to benchmark conditions, dataset, or methodology.
findings.suggestion: Add context: 'Based on testing with [conditions], batch processing typically outperforms streaming for files under 10MB'

findings.description: Deduplication window mechanics undefined — unclear how cache consistency is maintained in distributed system
findings.evidence: Line 21: 'deduplicates events within a 5-minute window using a Redis cache' — No explanation of window boundaries, cache coherency, or what happens at window edge.
findings.suggestion: Clarify: 'Events are deduplicated within 5-minute sliding windows; Redis cache stores fingerprints with automatic TTL-based expiration'

findings.description: Overflow queue mentioned but not explained — unclear what conditions trigger it or how it differs from primary path
findings.evidence: Line 25: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' — No definition of 'large' or operational model.
findings.suggestion: Add explanation: 'When input size exceeds [threshold], events route to overflow queue for [different processing model]'

findings.description: Tone shifts from technical detail (Stages 1-3) to operational platitudes (Performance and Conclusions)
findings.evidence: Stages 1-3 are precise and technical. Performance section uses vague language like 'Under nominal load' without defining conditions.
findings.suggestion: Maintain consistent technical tone throughout; either define 'nominal' or replace with specific conditions

findings.description: Operational Notes section is generic and vague regarding team responsibilities
findings.evidence: Line 48: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored by the on-call team via the Prometheus dashboard' — No explanation of escalation procedures or responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Add specificity: Define what 'on-call team' means, when they're engaged, and their response procedures
